steps.title: Find the enlargement factor
steps.content:
The new side length is 80% longer than the original: $$60 \text{ inches} \times (1 + 0.80) = 60 \times 1.8 = 108 \text{ inches}$$
The map has been enlarged by a linear factor of 1.8.

steps.title: Recognize the inverse scale relationship
steps.content:
The enlarged map represents the same geographic region as the original, just printed larger.

The original map's side length (60 inches) corresponds to $$60 \times 12 = 720 \text{ miles}$$
The enlarged map shows this same 720-mile region in 108 inches, so the new scale is $$\frac{720 \text{ miles}}{108 \text{ inches}} = \frac{12}{1.8} \text{ miles per inch}$$

steps.title: Calculate the new scale
steps.content: $$\frac{12}{1.8} = 6.666...$$, which rounds to 6.67 miles per inch. That is the correct answer.
